title: Background runtime strategy: beginBackgroundTask for T0, BGProcessing for T1+
Add charging state to CapabilitySnapshot and CapabilitiesService
description:
FOUNDATION: CapabilitiesService currently tracks thermal state and Low Power Mode but NOT charging state. The entire pre-analysis tier system depends on observing charging transitions -- T1+ work only runs while charging, and the scheduler needs to pause/resume when the charger is connected/disconnected.

WHY THIS IS FIRST: Every other bead in this feature depends on knowing whether the device is charging. Without this, the scheduler cannot make eligibility decisions for deferred-lane work.

WHAT TO DO:

1. Add isCharging: Bool to CapabilitySnapshot (Playhead/Services/Capabilities/CapabilitySnapshot.swift).

Add after isLowPowerMode (line 22) to keep the struct logically grouped:
    /// Whether the device is currently charging or fully charged.
    let isCharging: Bool

IMPORTANT: CapabilitySnapshot is Codable and existing JSON is persisted in the analysis_assets.capabilitySnapshot column. The struct currently uses synthesized Codable conformance (memberwise). Adding isCharging will break decoding of old persisted data that lacks this key. Add a custom init(from:) decoder:

    init(from decoder: Decoder) throws {
        let container = try decoder.container(keyedBy: CodingKeys.self)
        foundationModelsAvailable = try container.decode(Bool.self, forKey: .foundationModelsAvailable)
        appleIntelligenceEnabled = try container.decode(Bool.self, forKey: .appleIntelligenceEnabled)
        foundationModelsLocaleSupported = try container.decode(Bool.self, forKey: .foundationModelsLocaleSupported)
        thermalState = try container.decode(ThermalState.self, forKey: .thermalState)
        isLowPowerMode = try container.decode(Bool.self, forKey: .isLowPowerMode)
        isCharging = try container.decodeIfPresent(Bool.self, forKey: .isCharging) ?? false
        backgroundProcessingSupported = try container.decode(Bool.self, forKey: .backgroundProcessingSupported)
        availableDiskSpaceBytes = try container.decode(Int64.self, forKey: .availableDiskSpaceBytes)
        capturedAt = try container.decode(Date.self, forKey: .capturedAt)
    }

Also add the shouldDeferWork computed property (used by scheduler for deferred-lane gating):
    /// Whether deferred (T1+) work is allowed: charging and not thermally constrained.
    var canRunDeferredWork: Bool {
        isCharging && !shouldThrottleAnalysis
    }

2. Update CapabilitiesService.captureSnapshot() (Playhead/Services/Capabilities/CapabilitiesService.swift, line 108-129).

Change the static method to read battery state. Since captureSnapshot() is static, it cannot set isBatteryMonitoringEnabled (which requires a UIDevice instance). Instead, read the value assuming monitoring was enabled in startObserving(). On simulator, batteryState returns .unknown -- treat as not charging.

Replace the return statement at lines 119-129:
    let batteryState = UIDevice.current.batteryState
    let isCharging = batteryState == .charging || batteryState == .full

    return CapabilitySnapshot(
        foundationModelsAvailable: foundationModelsAvailable,
        appleIntelligenceEnabled: appleIntelligenceEnabled,
        foundationModelsLocaleSupported: localeSupported,
        thermalState: thermalState,
        isLowPowerMode: isLowPowerMode,
        isCharging: isCharging,
        backgroundProcessingSupported: backgroundProcessingSupported,
        availableDiskSpaceBytes: availableDiskSpace,
        capturedAt: .now
    )

Add import UIKit at the top of CapabilitiesService.swift (after import OSLog).

3. Enable battery monitoring ONCE in startObserving() (line 78-103).

Add at the top of startObserving(), BEFORE removeObservers():
    UIDevice.current.isBatteryMonitoringEnabled = true

Add batteryStateDidChangeNotification observer alongside existing thermal and power observers:
    let batteryToken = center.addObserver(
        forName: UIDevice.batteryStateDidChangeNotification,
        object: nil,
        queue: .main
    ) { [weak self] _ in
        guard let self else { return }
        Task { await self.refreshSnapshot() }
    }

    observerTokens = [thermalToken, powerToken, batteryToken]

4. Fix ALL existing call sites that construct CapabilitySnapshot manually.

Grep results show these files construct CapabilitySnapshot(...):
  - PlayheadTests/Services/CoreServiceTests.swift -- 10+ call sites at lines 1401, 1425, 1437, 1452, 1464, 1479, 1492.
    Add isCharging: false (or true where testing charging) to each construction.
  - Playhead/Services/Capabilities/CapabilitiesService.swift line 119 -- handled in step 2 above.

To find all sites: grep -rn 'CapabilitySnapshot(' Playhead/ PlayheadTests/ --include='*.swift'

5. Verify BackgroundProcessingService compatibility (Playhead/Services/AnalysisCoordinator/BackgroundProcessingService.swift).

BackgroundProcessingService.updateBatteryState() at line 392 sets UIDevice.current.isBatteryMonitoringEnabled = true on every call. With step 3 enabling it once in CapabilitiesService.startObserving(), this redundant set is harmless but wasteful. RECOMMENDATION: Remove the isBatteryMonitoringEnabled = true line from BackgroundProcessingService.updateBatteryState() (line 394) since CapabilitiesService now owns battery monitoring. BackgroundProcessingService should read from CapabilitiesService.currentSnapshot.isCharging instead of accessing UIDevice directly. However, this refactor is OPTIONAL for this bead -- the dual-set is safe, just redundant.

SIMULATOR NOTE: UIDevice.current.batteryState always returns .unknown on the simulator. captureSnapshot() treats .unknown as not charging (isCharging = false). Tests that need charging=true must construct CapabilitySnapshot directly with isCharging: true.

TESTING:
Run: xcodegen generate && xcodebuild test -scheme Playhead -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16 Pro' -only-testing PlayheadTests/CapabilitySnapshotTests 2>&1 | tail -20

Test cases (PlayheadTests/Services/Capabilities/CapabilitySnapshotTests.swift):
- testIsChargingFieldPresent: construct CapabilitySnapshot with isCharging=true, assert field value.
- testBackwardCompatDecoding: encode a CapabilitySnapshot JSON without isCharging key, decode it, assert isCharging==false (default).
- testCanRunDeferredWork: isCharging=true + thermalState=.nominal -> canRunDeferredWork==true. isCharging=true + thermalState=.serious -> canRunDeferredWork==false. isCharging=false -> canRunDeferredWork==false.
- testShouldThrottleAnalysisUnchanged: verify existing shouldThrottleAnalysis behavior is not broken (serious/critical -> true, nominal/fair -> false).
- testCapabilitiesServiceRefreshesOnBatteryChange: post UIDevice.batteryStateDidChangeNotification, verify refreshSnapshot() was called (use XCTestExpectation on capabilityUpdates stream).

FILES:
- Modify: Playhead/Services/Capabilities/CapabilitySnapshot.swift (add isCharging, custom init(from:), canRunDeferredWork)
- Modify: Playhead/Services/Capabilities/CapabilitiesService.swift (add UIKit import, battery monitoring in startObserving, read batteryState in captureSnapshot)
- Modify: PlayheadTests/Services/CoreServiceTests.swift (add isCharging: param to all CapabilitySnapshot constructions)
- Create: PlayheadTests/Services/Capabilities/CapabilitySnapshotTests.swift

title: Create shared test fixtures and service stubs for pre-analysis testing
description:
All pre-analysis beads reference test stubs that do not exist yet. The production services are concrete actors (AnalysisAudioService in AnalysisAudio.swift, AdDetectionService, CapabilitiesService, DownloadManager) with no protocol abstraction, so testing the AnalysisJobRunner, AnalysisWorkScheduler, SkipCueMaterializer, and AnalysisJobReconciler requires either (a) introducing protocols for each service or (b) building thin stub actors that replicate the interface. Option (a) is preferable for long-term testability.

WHAT TO DO:

1. Extract protocols from the four actor-based services used by AnalysisJobRunner:
   - AnalysisAudioProviding (from AnalysisAudioService in Playhead/Services/AnalysisAudio/AnalysisAudio.swift)
   - AdDetectionProviding (from AdDetectionService in Playhead/Services/AdDetection/AdDetectionService.swift)
   - CapabilitiesProviding (from CapabilitiesService in Playhead/Services/Capabilities/CapabilitiesService.swift)
   - DownloadProviding (from DownloadManager in Playhead/Services/Downloads/DownloadManager.swift) -- subset needed by scheduler/reconciler
   Each protocol declares only the methods called by the runner/scheduler. The existing actors conform to the protocol with no behavior change.

   Example for AnalysisAudioProviding:
     protocol AnalysisAudioProviding: Sendable {
         func decodeShards(audioURL: URL, range: Range<TimeInterval>) async throws -> [AnalysisShard]
     }
     extension AnalysisAudioService: AnalysisAudioProviding {}

   Example for DownloadProviding:
     protocol DownloadProviding: Sendable {
         func cachedFileURL(for episodeId: String) -> URL?
         func fingerprint(for episodeId: String) -> AudioFingerprint?
     }
     extension DownloadManager: DownloadProviding {}

   Look at the actual method signatures on each actor to determine which methods the runner/scheduler call. Grep for usages in AnalysisCoordinator.swift to see the call patterns.

2. Create stub implementations in a shared test support directory:
   - StubAnalysisAudioProvider: returns configurable shards array, can throw configurable errors.
   - StubAdDetectionProvider: returns configurable [AdWindow] array.
   - StubCapabilitiesProvider: returns configurable CapabilitySnapshot, has a settable currentSnapshot.
   - StubDownloadProvider: returns configurable cachedFileURL results and fingerprints.
   - StubAnalysisStore: wraps a real temp-dir AnalysisStore for test isolation (reuse the makeTestStore pattern from CoreServiceTests).

   Example stub:
     final class StubAnalysisAudioProvider: AnalysisAudioProviding, @unchecked Sendable {
         var shardsToReturn: [AnalysisShard] = []
         var errorToThrow: Error?

         func decodeShards(audioURL: URL, range: Range<TimeInterval>) async throws -> [AnalysisShard] {
             if let error = errorToThrow { throw error }
             return shardsToReturn
         }
     }

3. Promote existing private helpers to shared:
   - makeTempDir() and TestTempDirTracker already exist in PlayheadTests/Helpers/TestHelpers.swift -- no move needed, they are already shared.
   - Add makeTestStore() as a shared helper in TestHelpers.swift. Currently duplicated as a private function in CoreServiceTests.swift (line 136) and PipelineIntegrationTests.swift. Extract into TestHelpers.swift and remove the private copies.
   - Add makeShard() helper to TestHelpers.swift (currently private in TranscriptEngineTests).

   makeTestStore pattern (from CoreServiceTests.swift line 136):
     func makeTestStore() async throws -> AnalysisStore {
         let dir = try makeTempDir(prefix: "StoreTest")
         tempDirTracker.track(dir)
         return try AnalysisStore(directory: dir)
     }
   Note: tempDirTracker must be a file-level let in TestHelpers.swift:
     let tempDirTracker = TestTempDirTracker()

4. Add factory methods for test data (in PlayheadTests/Helpers/TestFactories.swift):
   - makeAnalysisJob(overrides:) -- creates an AnalysisJob with sensible defaults (jobId=UUID, state="queued", priority=0, desiredCoverageSec=90, etc.) and optional overrides for each field.
   - makeAnalysisRangeRequest(overrides:) -- creates an AnalysisRangeRequest with defaults (mode=.preRollWarmup, range=0..<90, etc.).
   - makeCapabilitySnapshot(isCharging:, thermalState:, isLowPowerMode:) -- for scheduler eligibility tests. All other fields use sensible defaults (foundationModelsAvailable=true, etc.).
   - makeAdWindow(startTime:, endTime:, confidence:) -- for materializer tests.

WHY A DEDICATED BEAD: Six other beads (bd-33a, bd-wbg, bd-2he, bd-ghg, bd-1tu, bd-26d) all specify test files that need stubs, but none of them create the stubs. Without this bead, the first implementer will create ad-hoc stubs in their test file, and subsequent beads will either duplicate them or have import failures.

TESTING:
Run: xcodegen generate && xcodebuild test -scheme Playhead -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16 Pro' -only-testing PlayheadTests/TestFixtureValidationTests 2>&1 | tail -20

Create a small PlayheadTests/Helpers/TestFixtureValidationTests.swift with:
- testStubAudioProviderConforms: verify StubAnalysisAudioProvider conforms to AnalysisAudioProviding.
- testStubAdDetectionProviderConforms: verify StubAdDetectionProvider conforms to AdDetectionProviding.
- testMakeTestStoreReturnsUsableStore: call makeTestStore(), insert a row, read it back.
- testMakeAnalysisJobDefaults: call makeAnalysisJob(), assert all fields have sensible values.
- testMakeCapabilitySnapshotOverrides: call makeCapabilitySnapshot(isCharging: true), assert isCharging == true and thermalState == .nominal (default).

TEST PATHS:
- PlayheadTests/Helpers/TestHelpers.swift (existing -- extend with makeTestStore, makeShard)
- PlayheadTests/Helpers/StubServices.swift (new -- all stub actors/classes)
- PlayheadTests/Helpers/TestFactories.swift (new -- factory methods for test data)
- PlayheadTests/Helpers/TestFixtureValidationTests.swift (new -- validates the fixtures work)

PROTOCOLS (in production code, one file per protocol):
- Playhead/Services/AnalysisAudio/AnalysisAudioProviding.swift
- Playhead/Services/AdDetection/AdDetectionProviding.swift
- Playhead/Services/Capabilities/CapabilitiesProviding.swift
- Playhead/Services/Downloads/DownloadProviding.swift

title: Observability: OSSignposter intervals and diagnostic metrics
description:
Add instrumentation for tuning and diagnostics. Without observability, this feature is impossible to tune by intuition.

SIGNPOSTER INTERVALS (OSSignposter):
- queue_wait: time from job enqueue to job start.
- job_duration: time from job start to outcome.
- download_to_cue: time from download completion to first SkipCue materialized.
- handoff_latency: time from playbackStarted() to lease transfer complete.
- thermal_pause: duration of thermal pauses.
- stage_duration: per-stage timing (spool, features, transcript, detect, materialize).

IMPLEMENTATION (Playhead/Services/PreAnalysis/PreAnalysisInstrumentation.swift):

  import OSLog

  enum PreAnalysisInstrumentation {
      static let signposter = OSSignposter(subsystem: "com.playhead", category: "PreAnalysis")
      static let logger = Logger(subsystem: "com.playhead", category: "PreAnalysis")

      // Signpost interval helpers
      static func beginQueueWait(jobId: String) -> OSSignpostIntervalState {
          signposter.beginInterval("queue_wait", id: .init(signposter), "\(jobId)")
      }
      static func endQueueWait(_ state: OSSignpostIntervalState) {
          signposter.endInterval("queue_wait", state)
      }
      // ... similar for job_duration, stage_duration, handoff_latency
  }

Where to add signpost calls:
- AnalysisWorkScheduler.enqueue(): beginQueueWait, store intervalState in job metadata or a local dictionary.
- AnalysisWorkScheduler.processJob(): endQueueWait at job start, begin/end job_duration around runner.run().
- AnalysisJobRunner.run(): begin/end stage_duration around each pipeline stage.
- AnalysisWorkScheduler.playbackStarted(): begin/end handoff_latency around lease transfer.

DIAGNOSTIC METRICS (logged at .info level, structured for grep-ability):
  PreAnalysisInstrumentation.logger.info("metric.cue_readiness episode=\(episodeId) ready=\(hadCues)")
  PreAnalysisInstrumentation.logger.info("metric.time_to_first_cue episode=\(episodeId) seconds=\(elapsed)")
  PreAnalysisInstrumentation.logger.info("metric.tier_completion tier=\(tier) completed=\(completed)")
  PreAnalysisInstrumentation.logger.info("metric.thermal_pause duration=\(seconds)")
  PreAnalysisInstrumentation.logger.info("metric.dedup_suppressed count=\(count)")

FEATURE FLAG: PreAnalysisConfig (created in bd-33a) has isEnabled, defaultT0DepthSeconds, t1/t2 depth fields, with UserDefaults persistence via load()/save(). The scheduler checks config.isEnabled before processing jobs. A debug settings screen can toggle isEnabled.

TESTING:
Run: xcodegen generate && xcodebuild test -scheme Playhead -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16 Pro' -only-testing PlayheadTests/InstrumentationTests 2>&1 | tail -20

Test cases (PlayheadTests/Services/PreAnalysis/InstrumentationTests.swift):
- testSignposterDoesNotCrash: call begin/end interval methods, verify no crash (cannot assert signpost content in unit tests, but verify API works). Use OSSignposter.disabled for unit test environments if needed.
- testFeatureFlagDisabledJobsStillCreated: with config.isEnabled=false, call scheduler.enqueue(), assert job exists in store with state=queued.
- testFeatureFlagDisabledSchedulerSkips: with config.isEnabled=false, start scheduler loop, assert no jobs processed after brief wait.
- testDiagnosticLoggerInitializes: verify PreAnalysisInstrumentation.logger is non-nil and can log without crash.

FILES:
- Create: Playhead/Services/PreAnalysis/PreAnalysisInstrumentation.swift
- Modify: Playhead/Services/PreAnalysis/AnalysisWorkScheduler.swift (add signposts at enqueue/processJob/playbackStarted, check config.isEnabled in loop)
- Modify: Playhead/Services/AnalysisJobRunner/AnalysisJobRunner.swift (add stage_duration signposts around each pipeline stage)
- Create: PlayheadTests/Services/PreAnalysis/InstrumentationTests.swift

title: Add analysis_jobs table to AnalysisStore (versioned job model)
description:
Replace the v1 PreAnalysisRecord with a versioned job model. The durable key is a workKey derived from fingerprint + analysisVersion + jobType, not just episodeId.

WHY VERSIONED JOBS: A single row keyed by episodeId cannot represent re-downloads, fingerprint changes, model availability changes, retries, or lease ownership. The workKey provides idempotency.

ARCHITECTURE WARNING -- SQLITE CONTENTION: AnalysisStore is opened with SQLITE_OPEN_NOMUTEX and relies on actor serialization for thread safety. ALL actors sharing this store instance call into the same actor-isolated methods, which means all SQLite writes are serialized through the AnalysisStore actor executor. This is correct for data integrity but creates a potential throughput bottleneck. For the new analysis_jobs CRUD, keep transactions short. The fetchNextEligibleJob query must be fast (indexed) to avoid holding the actor lock during scheduling decisions.

ANALYSISJOB STRUCT (add to AnalysisStore.swift after the PreviewBudget struct, around line 100):
  struct AnalysisJob: Sendable {
      let jobId: String           // UUID
      let jobType: String         // "preAnalysis" | "playback" | "backfill"
      let episodeId: String
      let podcastId: String?
      let analysisAssetId: String?
      let workKey: String         // fingerprint + analysisVersion + jobType
      let sourceFingerprint: String
      let downloadId: String
      let priority: Int           // higher = more important. 10 = explicit download, 0 = auto
      let desiredCoverageSec: Double
      let featureCoverageSec: Double
      let transcriptCoverageSec: Double
      let cueCoverageSec: Double
      let state: String           // queued | running | paused | complete | failed | superseded | blocked:*
      let attemptCount: Int
      let nextEligibleAt: Double? // nil = immediately eligible
      let leaseOwner: String?     // actor ID holding the lease
      let leaseExpiresAt: Double? // when lease expires
      let lastErrorCode: String?
      let createdAt: Double       // Date().timeIntervalSince1970
      let updatedAt: Double
  }

WORKKEY COMPUTATION:
  static func computeWorkKey(fingerprint: String, analysisVersion: Int, jobType: String) -> String {
      "\(fingerprint):\(analysisVersion):\(jobType)"
  }
  analysisVersion is a constant defined in the app (e.g., let kAnalysisVersion = 1). Increment on app updates that change detection logic.

SCHEMA (add to AnalysisStore.migrate(), after existing CREATE TABLE statements):
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS analysis_jobs (
      jobId TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      jobType TEXT NOT NULL,
      episodeId TEXT NOT NULL,
      podcastId TEXT,
      analysisAssetId TEXT,
      workKey TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
      sourceFingerprint TEXT NOT NULL,
      downloadId TEXT NOT NULL,
      priority INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      desiredCoverageSec REAL NOT NULL,
      featureCoverageSec REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      transcriptCoverageSec REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      cueCoverageSec REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      state TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'queued',
      attemptCount INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      nextEligibleAt REAL,
      leaseOwner TEXT,
      leaseExpiresAt REAL,
      lastErrorCode TEXT,
      createdAt REAL NOT NULL,
      updatedAt REAL NOT NULL
  );
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_jobs_state_priority ON analysis_jobs(state, priority DESC, createdAt ASC);
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_jobs_workkey ON analysis_jobs(workKey);
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_jobs_episode ON analysis_jobs(episodeId);

CRUD METHODS (add to AnalysisStore in a new // MARK: - AnalysisJob CRUD section):

  func insertJob(_ job: AnalysisJob) throws
    -- INSERT OR IGNORE INTO analysis_jobs VALUES(...)
    -- Returns silently if workKey already exists (idempotent enqueue)

  func fetchJob(byId jobId: String) throws -> AnalysisJob?
    -- SELECT * FROM analysis_jobs WHERE jobId = ?

  func fetchJob(byWorkKey workKey: String) throws -> AnalysisJob?
    -- SELECT * FROM analysis_jobs WHERE workKey = ?

  func fetchNextEligibleJob(isCharging: Bool, isThermalOk: Bool, t0ThresholdSec: Double, now: TimeInterval) throws -> AnalysisJob?
    -- The critical scheduling query. Must be fast (uses idx_jobs_state_priority).
    -- SQL (from bd-33a, inlined here for self-containment):
    SELECT * FROM analysis_jobs
    WHERE state IN ('queued', 'paused')
    AND (nextEligibleAt IS NULL OR nextEligibleAt <= :now)
    AND (leaseOwner IS NULL OR leaseExpiresAt < :now)
    AND (
      (desiredCoverageSec <= :t0ThresholdSec AND cueCoverageSec < desiredCoverageSec)
      OR
      (:isCharging = 1 AND :isThermalOk = 1)
    )
    ORDER BY priority DESC, createdAt ASC
    LIMIT 1

  func updateJobProgress(jobId: String, featureCoverageSec: Double, transcriptCoverageSec: Double, cueCoverageSec: Double) throws
    -- UPDATE analysis_jobs SET featureCoverageSec=?, transcriptCoverageSec=?, cueCoverageSec=?, updatedAt=? WHERE jobId=?

  func updateJobState(jobId: String, state: String, nextEligibleAt: Double? = nil, lastErrorCode: String? = nil) throws
    -- UPDATE analysis_jobs SET state=?, nextEligibleAt=?, lastErrorCode=?, attemptCount=attemptCount+(state='failed' ? 1 : 0), updatedAt=? WHERE jobId=?

  func acquireLease(jobId: String, owner: String, expiresAt: Double) throws -> Bool
    -- UPDATE analysis_jobs SET leaseOwner=?, leaseExpiresAt=?, state='running', updatedAt=? WHERE jobId=? AND (leaseOwner IS NULL OR leaseExpiresAt < :now)
    -- Returns true if sqlite3_changes() > 0 (lease acquired), false if already leased

  func releaseLease(jobId: String) throws
    -- UPDATE analysis_jobs SET leaseOwner=NULL, leaseExpiresAt=NULL, updatedAt=? WHERE jobId=?

  func renewLease(jobId: String, newExpiresAt: Double) throws
    -- UPDATE analysis_jobs SET leaseExpiresAt=?, updatedAt=? WHERE jobId=? AND leaseOwner IS NOT NULL

  func fetchJobsByState(_ state: String) throws -> [AnalysisJob]
    -- SELECT * FROM analysis_jobs WHERE state = ?

  func fetchJobsWithExpiredLeases(before: TimeInterval) throws -> [AnalysisJob]
    -- SELECT * FROM analysis_jobs WHERE state = 'running' AND leaseExpiresAt < ?

  func deleteOldJobs(olderThan: TimeInterval, inStates: [String]) throws -> Int
    -- DELETE FROM analysis_jobs WHERE state IN (...) AND updatedAt < ?

  func fetchAllJobEpisodeIds() throws -> Set<String>
    -- SELECT DISTINCT episodeId FROM analysis_jobs WHERE state NOT IN ('complete', 'superseded')

TESTING:
Run: xcodegen generate && xcodebuild test -scheme Playhead -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16 Pro' -only-testing PlayheadTests/AnalysisJobStoreTests 2>&1 | tail -20

Test cases (use makeTestStore() and makeAnalysisJob() from bd-1dl test fixtures):
- testInsertAndFetch: insert job, fetch by ID, assert all fields match.
- testWorkKeyDedup: insert two jobs with same workKey -> second insert silently ignored, only one row.
- testFetchNextEligibleJobT0: insert T0 job (desiredCoverage=90, cueCoverage=0), assert returned by fetchNextEligibleJob with isCharging=false.
- testFetchNextEligibleJobDeferredRequiresCharging: insert T1 job (desiredCoverage=300), call with isCharging=false -> returns nil. Call with isCharging=true -> returns the job.
- testPriorityOrdering: insert priority=0 and priority=10 jobs, assert priority=10 returned first.
- testNextEligibleAtRespected: insert job with nextEligibleAt in the future -> not returned. Advance time past it -> returned.
- testLeaseAcquireRelease: acquire lease, verify leaseOwner set. Release, verify cleared.
- testLeaseConflict: acquire lease on job, attempt second acquire -> returns false.
- testUpdateProgress: update coverage fields, fetch, assert new values.
- testUpdateState: transition queued -> running -> complete, verify state column.
- testMigrationCreatesTable: fresh store, verify analysis_jobs table exists via sqlite3_exec("SELECT 1 FROM analysis_jobs LIMIT 0").

FILES:
- Modify: Playhead/Persistence/AnalysisStore/AnalysisStore.swift (AnalysisJob struct, DDL, CRUD methods)
- Create: PlayheadTests/Services/AnalysisJobStoreTests.swift

title: Integration test suite for pre-analysis feature
description:
Comprehensive integration tests covering the scenarios identified in review. These go beyond unit tests and verify cross-component behavior.

STUB DEPENDENCIES: Use the shared stubs from bd-1dl (PlayheadTests/Helpers/StubServices.swift): StubAnalysisAudioProvider, StubAdDetectionProvider, StubCapabilitiesProvider, StubDownloadProvider. Use makeTestStore() from TestHelpers.swift and factory methods from TestFactories.swift. Do NOT create separate mocks -- the naming convention is Stub*, not Mock*.

If any test needs a spy (recording calls for assertion), extend the existing Stub with a callLog array rather than creating a parallel Mock class.

TEST SCENARIOS:
1. Duplicate enqueue: same episode, same fingerprint -> no duplicate job (workKey dedup).
   Setup: call scheduler.enqueue() twice with identical params. Assert store has exactly 1 job.

2. Re-download with new fingerprint: new content version -> old job superseded, new job created with new workKey.
   Setup: enqueue job A with fingerprint "abc". Enqueue job B for same episodeId with fingerprint "xyz". Assert job A state=superseded, job B state=queued.

3. Paused T1 does NOT block eligible T0: enqueue T1-paused and T0-pending, verify T0 is selected by fetchNextEligibleJob.
   Setup: insert T1 job (desiredCoverage=300, state=paused) and T0 job (desiredCoverage=90, state=queued). Call fetchNextEligibleJob(isCharging=false). Assert T0 returned.

4. Model becomes available mid-queue: job in blocked:modelUnavailable -> reconciler unblocks -> becomes eligible when model installed.
   Setup: insert job with state=blocked:modelUnavailable. Configure StubCapabilitiesProvider with canUseFoundationModels=true. Run reconciler.reconcile(). Assert job state=queued.

5. Download completes while app is backgrounded: verify reconciler picks up un-enqueued download on launch.
   Setup: configure StubDownloadProvider with cachedFileURL for episodeId "ep1". No job in store for "ep1". Run reconciler.reconcile(). Assert new job created for "ep1".

6. User taps Play during background job commit: lease transfer flow.
   Setup: insert running job with active lease. Call scheduler.playbackStarted(episodeId:). Assert lease released within 3s. Then call skipOrchestrator.beginEpisode() and assert pre-materialized cues are loaded.

7. Overlap between preloaded cues and live-generated cues: same ad region detected by both pre-analysis and live detection -> one SkipCue (cueHash dedup).
   Setup: materialize SkipCue for time range 30-60. Materialize again with same range from "live" source. Assert only 1 SkipCue in store (UNIQUE cueHash constraint).

8. Charger removed mid-T2: scheduler detects charging=false via capability observer -> cancels deferred-lane job -> job checkpoints partial progress -> on reconnect, job resumes from frontier.
   Setup: start T2 job with StubCapabilitiesProvider (isCharging=true). Mid-run, update stub to isCharging=false and yield new snapshot. Assert job paused with partial progress. Update to isCharging=true. Assert job resumes from frontier.

9. Stale job after app upgrade: version reconciliation supersedes old job, creates replacement with new workKey.
   Setup: insert job with old analysisVersion. Run reconciler with current version. Assert old job superseded.

10. Audio file evicted by LRU while job is paused: blocked:missingFile -> reconciler checks cachedFileURL -> unblocked if re-cached, stays blocked if not.
    Setup: insert job with state=blocked:missingFile. Configure StubDownloadProvider to return nil -> stays blocked. Then configure to return URL -> reconciler unblocks.

11. T0 depth podcast-profile adjustment: enqueue with podcastId that has long pre-roll history -> desiredCoverage > 90s. Enqueue with clean-feed podcast -> desiredCoverage < 90s.
    Setup: requires PodcastProfile in store with adjusted values. This validates the integration path between PodcastProfile and scheduler enqueue.

12. Feature flag disabled: enqueue creates jobs but scheduler does not process them. Re-enable -> jobs processed.
    Setup: create scheduler with PreAnalysisConfig(isEnabled: false). Call enqueue(). Assert job in store with state=queued. Run scheduler loop. Assert no jobs processed. Set isEnabled=true. Run loop. Assert job processed.

13. Exponential backoff: job fails 3 times -> nextEligibleAt follows 60s, 120s, 240s progression.
    Setup: configure StubAdDetectionProvider to throw errors. Run scheduler 3 times. After each failure, assert nextEligibleAt matches expected backoff.

14. Lease expiry without completion (crash simulation): job in running state, lease expired -> reconciler requeues on next launch.
    Setup: insert job with state=running, leaseExpiresAt in the past. Run reconciler.reconcile(). Assert state=queued, attemptCount incremented.

Each test follows the pattern:

2. Create component instances with stubs
3. Execute the scenario
4. Assert final SQLite state via store queries

title: Create SkipCue materialization layer
description:
Raw AdWindow rows are internal detection artifacts. Separately materialize SkipCue rows as the user-safe cue layer. Solves dedup when live detection re-emits overlapping windows, and confidence-threshold gating.

ARCHITECTURE NOTE -- CLEAN BOUNDARY WITH SKIP ORCHESTRATOR: The SkipCueMaterializer transforms detection-side AdWindows into playback-side SkipCues. This is the correct architectural boundary -- it separates detection concerns from skip-policy concerns. The materializer should be a plain struct (not an actor) since it only does stateless transforms + store writes. Making it an actor would add unnecessary hop overhead.

ARCHITECTURE NOTE -- DEDUP BETWEEN PRE-ANALYSIS AND LIVE: When playback starts, SkipOrchestrator.beginEpisode() will load pre-materialized SkipCues. If the live pipeline (AnalysisCoordinator) then detects the same ad region, the cueHash dedup prevents duplicates. This is correct but requires that both paths use identical rounding for cueHash computation.

CUE HASH ROUNDING RULE (used by both pre-analysis and live paths):
  cueHash = SHA256("\(analysisAssetId):\(Int(startTime)):\(Int(endTime))")
  startTime and endTime are rounded DOWN to 1-second granularity via Int() truncation. This means two AdWindows at 12.3-45.7 and 12.8-45.2 produce the same cueHash ("...:12:45") and are deduplicated. Document this prominently in the SkipCueMaterializer source.

SKIPCUE STRUCT (add to AnalysisStore.swift after the AdWindow struct, around line 86):
  struct SkipCue: Sendable {
      let id: String          // UUID
      let analysisAssetId: String
      let cueHash: String     // dedup key (see rounding rule above)
      let startTime: Double
      let endTime: Double
      let confidence: Double  // from source AdWindow
      let source: String      // "preAnalysis" | "live"
      let materializedAt: Double // Date().timeIntervalSince1970
      let wasSkipped: Bool    // updated by SkipOrchestrator when user reaches this cue
      let userDismissed: Bool // updated if user taps "don't skip"
  }

DDL (add to AnalysisStore.migrate() after the ad_windows CREATE TABLE):
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS skip_cues (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      analysisAssetId TEXT NOT NULL,
      cueHash TEXT NOT NULL,
      startTime REAL NOT NULL,
      endTime REAL NOT NULL,
      confidence REAL NOT NULL,
      source TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'preAnalysis',
      materializedAt REAL NOT NULL,
      wasSkipped INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      userDismissed INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      UNIQUE(cueHash)  -- dedup constraint
  );
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_skip_cues_asset ON skip_cues(analysisAssetId);
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_skip_cues_time ON skip_cues(analysisAssetId, startTime);

CRUD METHODS (add to AnalysisStore, in a new // MARK: - SkipCue CRUD section):
  func insertSkipCue(_ cue: SkipCue) throws
    -- INSERT OR IGNORE (the UNIQUE(cueHash) constraint handles dedup silently)
  func insertSkipCues(_ cues: [SkipCue]) throws
    -- Batch insert in a BEGIN/COMMIT transaction for performance
  func fetchSkipCues(for analysisAssetId: String) throws -> [SkipCue]
    -- SELECT * FROM skip_cues WHERE analysisAssetId = ? ORDER BY startTime ASC
  func markSkipCueSkipped(id: String) throws
    -- UPDATE skip_cues SET wasSkipped = 1 WHERE id = ?
  func markSkipCueDismissed(id: String) throws
    -- UPDATE skip_cues SET userDismissed = 1 WHERE id = ?

SKIPCUEMATERIALIZER IMPLEMENTATION (Playhead/Services/SkipOrchestrator/SkipCueMaterializer.swift):

  import CryptoKit
  import Foundation

  struct SkipCueMaterializer {
      private let store: AnalysisStore
      private let confidenceThreshold: Double

      init(store: AnalysisStore, confidenceThreshold: Double = 0.7) {
          self.store = store
          self.confidenceThreshold = confidenceThreshold
      }

      /// Transform AdWindows into SkipCues and persist. Returns count of NEW cues inserted.
      func materialize(adWindows: [AdWindow], analysisAssetId: String, source: String) async throws -> Int {
          let cues = adWindows
              .filter { $0.confidence >= confidenceThreshold }
              .map { window in
                  SkipCue(
                      id: UUID().uuidString,
                      analysisAssetId: analysisAssetId,
                      cueHash: Self.computeCueHash(assetId: analysisAssetId, start: window.startTime, end: window.endTime),
                      startTime: window.startTime,
                      endTime: window.endTime,
                      confidence: window.confidence,
                      source: source,
                      materializedAt: Date().timeIntervalSince1970,
                      wasSkipped: false,
                      userDismissed: false
                  )
              }
          let beforeCount = try await store.fetchSkipCues(for: analysisAssetId).count
          try await store.insertSkipCues(cues)
          let afterCount = try await store.fetchSkipCues(for: analysisAssetId).count
          return afterCount - beforeCount
      }

      /// Deterministic cue hash for dedup. Uses 1-second granularity rounding (Int truncation).
      static func computeCueHash(assetId: String, start: Double, end: Double) -> String {
          let input = "\(assetId):\(Int(start)):\(Int(end))"
          let digest = SHA256.hash(data: Data(input.utf8))
          return digest.map { String(format: "%02x", $0) }.joined()
      }
  }

SKIPORCHESTRATOR PRELOAD (modify Playhead/Services/SkipOrchestrator/SkipOrchestrator.swift):
In the existing beginEpisode() method, add preloading of pre-materialized SkipCues BEFORE existing live AdWindow loading. The SkipOrchestrator needs a reference to AnalysisStore (add as an init parameter if not already present). Add this at the top of beginEpisode:
  let preCues = try await store.fetchSkipCues(for: analysisAssetId)
  // Convert to orchestrator's internal cue representation and merge into activeCues

TESTING:
Run: xcodegen generate && xcodebuild test -scheme Playhead -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16 Pro' -only-testing PlayheadTests/SkipCueMaterializerTests 2>&1 | tail -20

Test cases for SkipCueMaterializerTests.swift:
- testMaterializeAboveThreshold: 3 AdWindows (confidence 0.8, 0.9, 0.5), threshold 0.7 -> 2 SkipCues inserted.
- testDedup: materialize same AdWindows twice -> second call returns 0 new cues, total count unchanged.
- testCueHashRounding: AdWindow at 12.3-45.7 and 12.8-45.2 produce same cueHash.
- testBelowThresholdFiltered: all AdWindows below threshold -> 0 cues.
- testSourceTagging: materialize with source="preAnalysis" -> all cues have source="preAnalysis".

Test cases for SkipOrchestratorPreloadTests.swift:
- testBeginEpisodeLoadsCues: insert SkipCues into store, call beginEpisode, assert orchestrator has them.
- testBeginEpisodeWithNoCues: no pre-materialized cues -> orchestrator starts empty (existing behavior).
- testLiveDedupWithPreloaded: pre-materialize a cue, then simulate live detection of same region -> no duplicate.

All tests use makeTestStore() from PlayheadTests/Helpers/TestHelpers.swift (shared helper created by bd-1dl).

title: Create AnalysisWorkScheduler with eligibility-aware job selection
Reconciler: blocked states, backoff, and job repair
description:
Add a reconciler that runs at each reconciliation point to repair stale/broken job state. Production background systems need explicit recovery.

ARCHITECTURE NOTE -- LEASE vs ACTOR COORDINATION: The reconciler exists partly because the lease model pushes coordination into SQLite. If leases were replaced with actor-level coordination, several reconciler responsibilities simplify: leaseLost recovery becomes unnecessary (actor state is authoritative), and the crash-recovery path only needs to check for jobs in running state at launch (no lease expiry math). The reconciler is still needed for missingFile, modelUnavailable, and versionStale cases.

ARCHITECTURE WARNING -- RECONCILER STORE CONTENTION: reconcile() performs multiple queries and updates in sequence. During reconciliation, the store actor is busy handling these requests, which delays any concurrent pipeline writes from AnalysisCoordinator or AnalysisJobRunner. Reconciliation should run at low-activity times (launch, before scheduler loop starts). Batch updates where possible rather than issuing individual UPDATE statements per job.

RECONCILER ACTOR:
  actor AnalysisJobReconciler {
      private let store: AnalysisStore
      private let downloadManager: any DownloadProviding  // protocol from bd-1dl
      private let logger = Logger(subsystem: "com.playhead", category: "JobReconciler")

      init(store: AnalysisStore, downloadManager: any DownloadProviding, capabilitiesService: any CapabilitiesProviding)

      /// Run full reconciliation pass. Called at launch, after model install, after background URL session completes.
      func reconcile() async throws -> ReconciliationReport
  }

  struct ReconciliationReport: Sendable {
      let expiredLeasesRecovered: Int
      let missingFilesUnblocked: Int
      let missingFilesStillBlocked: Int
      let modelsUnblocked: Int
      let staleVersionsSuperseded: Int
      let completedJobsGarbageCollected: Int
      let failedJobsBackedOff: Int
      let unEnqueuedDownloadsCreated: Int
  }

BLOCKED STATES (stored in analysis_jobs.state column):
  - "blocked:missingFile" -- audio file evicted by LRU cache. Set by scheduler when cachedFileURL returns nil.
  - "blocked:modelUnavailable" -- Whisper/detection model not downloaded. Set by runner when model load fails.
  - "blocked:versionStale" -- analysisVersion changed (app upgrade). Set by reconciler during version check.
  - "blocked:leaseLost" -- crash recovery. Set by reconciler when lease expired without clean completion.

RECONCILE() LOGIC (execute steps in order):

Step 1 -- Recover expired leases:
  SELECT * FROM analysis_jobs WHERE state = 'running' AND leaseExpiresAt < :now
  For each: UPDATE state = 'queued', leaseOwner = NULL, leaseExpiresAt = NULL, attemptCount = attemptCount + 1
  These are jobs that were running when the app crashed or was killed.

Step 2 -- Unblock missingFile jobs:
  SELECT * FROM analysis_jobs WHERE state = 'blocked:missingFile'
  For each: check downloadManager.cachedFileURL(for: job.episodeId)
    If file exists now: UPDATE state = 'queued', nextEligibleAt = NULL
    If still missing: leave as blocked:missingFile (no change)

Step 3 -- Unblock modelUnavailable jobs:
  SELECT * FROM analysis_jobs WHERE state = 'blocked:modelUnavailable'
  For each: check if the required model is now available (via CapabilitiesService.canUseFoundationModels or a model inventory check)
    If available: UPDATE state = 'queued', nextEligibleAt = NULL
    If still unavailable: leave as blocked:modelUnavailable

Step 4 -- Supersede versionStale jobs:
  Compare current app analysisVersion (from Bundle.main CFBundleShortVersionString or a constant) with each job's workKey version component.
  SELECT * FROM analysis_jobs WHERE state NOT IN ('complete', 'superseded') AND analysisVersion != :currentVersion
  For each: UPDATE state = 'superseded'. Optionally create a replacement job with new workKey if the episode still exists in the library.

Step 5 -- GC old completed/superseded jobs:
  DELETE FROM analysis_jobs WHERE state IN ('complete', 'superseded') AND updatedAt < :now - 604800
  (7-day retention for completed jobs, for diagnostic purposes)

Step 6 -- Apply exponential backoff to failed jobs:
  SELECT * FROM analysis_jobs WHERE state = 'failed' AND nextEligibleAt IS NULL
  For each: UPDATE nextEligibleAt = :now + min(2^attemptCount * 60, 3600)
  Backoff schedule: attempt 1 = 60s, 2 = 120s, 3 = 240s, 4 = 480s, ..., capped at 3600s (1 hour).

Step 7 -- Discover un-enqueued downloads:
  Query DownloadManager for all cached episodeIds (via a method like allCachedEpisodeIds() -- may need to add this to DownloadProviding protocol).
  For each cached episodeId: check if an analysis_jobs row exists with matching episodeId and state NOT IN ('complete', 'superseded').
  If no job exists: create a new job with state='queued', priority=0 (auto-download priority), desiredCoverageSec=90 (T0 default).
  This catches downloads that completed while the app was backgrounded and the scheduler never got the enqueue callback.

STORE METHODS NEEDED (add to AnalysisStore if not already present from bd-25o):
  func fetchJobsByState(_ state: String) throws -> [AnalysisJob]
  func fetchJobsWithExpiredLeases(before: TimeInterval) throws -> [AnalysisJob]
  func updateJobState(jobId: String, state: String, nextEligibleAt: TimeInterval?) throws
  func batchUpdateJobState(jobIds: [String], state: String, nextEligibleAt: TimeInterval?) throws
  func deleteOldJobs(olderThan: TimeInterval, inStates: [String]) throws -> Int
  func fetchAllJobEpisodeIds() throws -> Set<String>

TESTING:
Run: xcodegen generate && xcodebuild test -scheme Playhead -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16 Pro' -only-testing PlayheadTests/AnalysisJobReconcilerTests 2>&1 | tail -20

Test cases (all use makeTestStore() and StubDownloadProvider from bd-1dl):
- testRecoverExpiredLease: insert job with state=running, leaseExpiresAt in the past -> after reconcile, state=queued, attemptCount incremented.
- testUnblockMissingFileWhenRecached: insert job with state=blocked:missingFile, configure StubDownloadProvider to return a URL -> after reconcile, state=queued.
- testMissingFileStaysBlockedWhenStillMissing: insert blocked:missingFile job, StubDownloadProvider returns nil -> state unchanged.
- testSupersedeStaleVersion: insert job with old analysisVersion -> after reconcile, state=superseded.
- testGarbageCollectOldCompleted: insert completed job with updatedAt 8 days ago -> after reconcile, job deleted.
- testExponentialBackoff: insert failed job with attemptCount=3 -> after reconcile, nextEligibleAt = now + 480s.
- testBackoffCappedAt3600: insert failed job with attemptCount=10 -> nextEligibleAt = now + 3600 (not 2^10*60).
- testDiscoverUnEnqueuedDownload: configure StubDownloadProvider with cached episodeId not in store -> after reconcile, new job created.
- testReconciliationReport: run reconcile with mixed state -> verify report counts match.
- testIdempotent: run reconcile twice -> second pass has zero changes (report all zeros).

title: Wire AnalysisWorkScheduler into PlayheadRuntime composition root
description:
Connect all the pieces in the app composition root. PlayheadRuntime creates and wires AnalysisJobRunner, AnalysisWorkScheduler, AnalysisJobReconciler, and connects them to the existing DownloadManager.

ARCHITECTURE WARNING -- DUAL PIPELINE DURING V1: For v1, AnalysisCoordinator handles live playback analysis while AnalysisWorkScheduler handles background pre-analysis. They share the same AnalysisStore and AnalysisAsset rows via the lease system. This means: (1) Both can write to the same analysis_assets row (coordinator updates analysisState, scheduler updates via job progress). Ensure they write to different columns or use the lease to prevent concurrent writes to the same asset. (2) SkipOrchestrator.beginEpisode() must load cues from BOTH sources (pre-materialized SkipCues + any live-detected AdWindows). (3) The coordinator should eventually delegate its pipeline to AnalysisJobRunner -- design the runner API to support this from day one even if the coordinator refactor is deferred.

ARCHITECTURE NOTE -- ORDERING CONSTRAINT: playbackStarted() must be called BEFORE skipOrchestrator.beginEpisode() so that lease transfer completes and pre-analysis cues are finalized before the orchestrator loads them. This ordering is fragile -- add a comment at the call site.

WHAT TO DO in PlayheadRuntime.swift:

1. Add new stored properties (after line 25, alongside existing service declarations):
    let analysisJobRunner: AnalysisJobRunner
    let analysisWorkScheduler: AnalysisWorkScheduler
    let analysisJobReconciler: AnalysisJobReconciler

2. Initialize in init() (after downloadManager creation at line 117, before the error state check at line 120):
    self.analysisJobRunner = AnalysisJobRunner(
        store: analysisStore,
        audioService: audioService,      // conforms to AnalysisAudioProviding
        featureService: featureService,
        transcriptEngine: transcriptEngine,
        adDetectionService: adDetectionService  // conforms to AdDetectionProviding
    )
    self.analysisWorkScheduler = AnalysisWorkScheduler(
        store: analysisStore,
        jobRunner: analysisJobRunner,
        capabilitiesService: capabilitiesService,
        downloadManager: downloadManager
    )
    self.analysisJobReconciler = AnalysisJobReconciler(
        store: analysisStore,
        downloadManager: downloadManager,  // conforms to DownloadProviding
        capabilitiesService: capabilitiesService  // conforms to CapabilitiesProviding
    )

3. Wire DownloadManager -> Scheduler, then reconcile, then start loop (after init, in a new Task):
    Task {
        await downloadManager.setAnalysisWorkScheduler(analysisWorkScheduler)
        // Version check + reconciliation before starting scheduler
        let currentVersion = Bundle.main.infoDictionary?["CFBundleShortVersionString"] as? String ?? "0"
        let lastVersion = UserDefaults.standard.string(forKey: "lastAnalysisVersion") ?? "0"
        if currentVersion != lastVersion {
            try? await analysisJobReconciler.reconcile()
            UserDefaults.standard.set(currentVersion, forKey: "lastAnalysisVersion")
        }
        await analysisWorkScheduler.startSchedulerLoop()
    }

4. In the playEpisode() method (wherever it calls beginEpisode on skipOrchestrator), add playbackStarted BEFORE beginEpisode:
    // ORDERING: must come before beginEpisode so lease is released and cues are finalized
    await analysisWorkScheduler.playbackStarted(episodeId: episodeId)
    await skipOrchestrator.beginEpisode(episodeId: episodeId, analysisAssetId: assetId)

5. In the stopEpisode()/cleanup path, no scheduler changes needed -- the scheduler ignores stopped episodes.

TESTING:
This bead is wiring-only. Verify by running the app and checking:
- No crash on launch (all init dependencies resolve)
- Console logs show "Capability snapshot captured" (existing) and scheduler loop start (new)
- Download an episode -> console shows scheduler enqueue log

For automated verification:
Run: xcodegen generate && xcodebuild build -scheme Playhead -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16 Pro' 2>&1 | tail -20
(Build, not test -- this is a wiring bead. Integration tests cover behavior in bd-26d.)

- Modify: Playhead/App/PlayheadRuntime.swift (add properties, init wiring, reconciliation sequence, playbackStarted ordering)

title: Wire DownloadManager to AnalysisWorkScheduler
description:
Connect download completion to pre-analysis job creation. Minimal change to DownloadManager -- add DownloadContext and a scheduler reference.

EXISTING STATE: DownloadManager is initialized at PlayheadRuntime. progressiveDownload() is defined at DownloadManager.swift line 231. Fingerprint computation happens at lines 287-313 (weak fingerprint from HTTP metadata at lines 287-299, strong fingerprint from SHA-256 at line 312). cachedFileURL(for:) is at line 610.

WHAT TO DO:

1. Add DownloadContext struct to DownloadManager.swift (at the top, after the existing error enum, or in a separate file Playhead/Services/Downloads/DownloadContext.swift):
  struct DownloadContext: Sendable {
      let podcastId: String?
      let isExplicitDownload: Bool
  }

2. Add optional scheduler reference to DownloadManager:
  /// Set after PlayheadRuntime creates the scheduler (scheduler depends on DownloadManager, so it's created after).
  private var analysisWorkScheduler: AnalysisWorkScheduler?

  func setAnalysisWorkScheduler(_ scheduler: AnalysisWorkScheduler) {
      self.analysisWorkScheduler = scheduler
  }

3. Add optional DownloadContext parameter to progressiveDownload() -- default nil for backward compat:
  func progressiveDownload(
      episodeId: String,
      from url: URL,
      context: DownloadContext? = nil  // <-- add this parameter
  ) async throws -> URL {
  Existing callers (PlayheadRuntime) compile without changes because of the default nil.

4. After performDownload() completes fingerprint computation (after line 312 where strongHash is computed), if scheduler is set, enqueue:
  // After line 317 (logger.info line), add:
  if let scheduler = analysisWorkScheduler {
      await scheduler.enqueue(
          episodeId: episodeId,
          podcastId: context?.podcastId,
          downloadId: episodeId,  // cachedFileURL(for:) takes episodeId as key
          sourceFingerprint: strongHash,
          isExplicitDownload: context?.isExplicitDownload ?? false,
          desiredCoverage: nil  // let scheduler use default T0 policy
      )
  }

IMPORTANT: downloadId == episodeId in this codebase. The cachedFileURL(for episodeId:) method at line 610 takes an episodeId and returns the cached file path. The scheduler resolves audio URLs via downloadManager.cachedFileURL(for: job.downloadId), so downloadId must match the episodeId used for caching.

5. For background download delegate path (EpisodeDownloadDelegate): cannot call scheduler directly because the delegate runs in a non-actor context and may be invoked when the app is relaunching. Instead, the reconciler handles this on next launch by scanning for cached audio files without corresponding analysis jobs (step 7 of bd-ghg reconciler logic).

TESTING:
Run: xcodegen generate && xcodebuild test -scheme Playhead -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16 Pro' -only-testing PlayheadTests/DownloadManagerPreAnalysisTests 2>&1 | tail -20

Test cases (PlayheadTests/Services/Downloads/DownloadManagerPreAnalysisTests.swift):
- testDownloadContextConstruction: create DownloadContext with podcastId and isExplicitDownload, assert fields.
- testProgressiveDownloadWithoutContextCompiles: call progressiveDownload(episodeId:from:) without context param -> compiles and works (backward compat).
- testSchedulerCalledAfterDownload: set a spy scheduler (record enqueue calls), complete a download, assert enqueue was called with correct episodeId and sourceFingerprint.
- testSchedulerNilNoError: complete download with scheduler not set -> no crash, no enqueue call.
- testExplicitDownloadContext: pass DownloadContext(podcastId: "pod1", isExplicitDownload: true), verify scheduler.enqueue receives isExplicitDownload=true.

Note: testing the actual download requires either a mock URLSession or a local HTTP server. For unit testing the enqueue wiring, you can extract the post-download logic into a testable method or use a spy scheduler.

title: Create AnalysisJobRunner actor (bounded-range analysis engine)
